titles.title: Chitose-kun wa Ramune Bin no Naka
Chitose-kun is Inside a Ramune Bottle
Ramune no Bin ni Shizunda Biidama no Tsuki
千歳くんはラムネ瓶のなか
Chitose Is in the Ramune Bottle

data.title: Chitose-kun wa Ramune Bin no Naka
data.title_english: Chitose Is in the Ramune Bottle
data.title_japanese: 千歳くんはラムネ瓶のなか
title_synonyms: Chitose-kun is Inside a Ramune Bottle
Ramune no Bin ni Shizunda Biidama no Tsuki
data.synopsis:
Saku Chitose appears to lead an enviable high school life. Extraordinarily charismatic, tremendously confident, and effortlessly friendly, he is the kind of person others naturally look up to. But his popularity carries its own burdens; not everyone views him kindly, and whispers of cynicism follow wherever he shines too brightly. To Saku, living an ugly life is worse than death—a belief that drives his need to maintain his perfect reputation.

At the start of his second year, Saku's homeroom teacher asks him to help bring back a classmate who has stopped attending school. Initially hoping to resolve the matter quickly, Saku finds out that the shut-in Kenta Yamazaki harbors open contempt for him and everything he represents. A simple favor instead exposes the distance between those who live within the social light and those who were never given a chance to shine.

Surrounded by the expectant gazes of those who both admire and despise him, Saku treads the delicate line between sincerity and performance—searching for what it truly means to live beautifully in a world that sees only the surface.

[Written by MAL Rewrite]

titles.title: マゾメスソープで逢いましょう!

data.title: Mazome Soap de Aimashou!
data.title_japanese: マゾメスソープで逢いましょう!
data.synopsis:
Mitsurugi and Nagihara, the new employees at the company, are working diligently and are appreciated for their professional qualities. Mitsurugi is responsible, while Nagihara is admired for her refined behavior. However, secretly, both women work at a masochistic soapland, showing a completely different personality from what they display at work.

(Source: TMDB)

titles.title: 河鍋暁斎画「新富座妖怪引幕」

data.title: Kawanabe Kyousai: Shintomiza Youkai Hikimaku
data.title_japanese: 河鍋暁斎画「新富座妖怪引幕」
data.synopsis: Animated version of the classic Kawanabe Kyosai kabuki theatre curtain painting: popular actors of their time, depicted as yokai, or demons, are jumping out of a bamboo box and heading for the audience.

data.title: Oni to Yanari
data.synopsis:
This house rattling spirit has lived in this old house for generations. But the old woman who lives there as the final resident can no  longer hear the noises it makes, which leaves the house rattler filled with a sense of longing.

(Source: Official website)
data.background: Recipient in the 21st Digicon6 Asia Awards in categories Silver - Innovative Art and Regional / Japan - Silver.

data.title: Kanjigram
data.synopsis:
A video work inspired by the "meaning, form and origin" of kanji, reinterpreted and archived using the technique of stop-motion animation. A project that began with the realization that it is the "likeness" drawn out by the imagination that is the essence of kanji.

(Source: TECARAT's Official Site)
